channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: L’Oréal’s new cosmetics counter device can predict your skin’s needs
item.description: Choosing skincare products often comes down to a matter of trial and error. A tabletop device unveiled by L’Oréal, called the L’Oréal Cell BioPrint, is intended to change that.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Limits of Siloed AI Implementations: Why a Holistic Approach is Key to Unlocking Enterprise Value
item.description: Despite the promise of AI, many organizations are struggling with implementations that aren't delivering as hoped. Research from Gartner shows that 30% of generative AI will not make it past the proof-of-concept phase by the end of 2025. The chief issue is that enterprises often implement AI in siloes, missing opportunities to drive transformative outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NASA's Martian helicopter promises unprecedented views of the red planet
item.description: NASA's Ingenuity helicopter, which flew on Mars in April 2021, became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on a planet other than Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dating Wrapped: TikTok users are crunching the numbers on their dating life
item.description: If Spotify Wrapped left you underwhelmed this year, TikTok’s “Dating Wrapped” trend is here to spice things up. Instead of playlists, users are serving up PowerPoint-style presentations packed with stats about their 2024 dating lives.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Dating Sunday 2025: The busiest day on dating apps is almost here
item.description: Dating apps are gearing up for their busiest day of the year: Dating Sunday. This landmark day in the dating world always lands on the first Sunday of January.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk stays on brand following Tesla Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Elon Musk used his X account to defend the durability of Tesla's Cybertruck after the New Year's Las Vegas explosion.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as Meta global policy leader
item.description: Meta will switch up its global policy team in the new year and has appointed former George W. Bush administration official Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as the company's chief global affairs officer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Fine-tuning Azure OpenAI models in Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) like the OpenAI models used by Azure are general-purpose tools for building many different types of generative AI-powered applications, from chatbots to agent-powered workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry
item.description: Our government needs to maintain trust through competence, and that competence will increasingly be about powering government services with AI. The post The US government must keep pace with serving an AI-empowered citizenry first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hackers have posted some RIBridges data on the dark web, McKee says
item.description: The state disclosed earlier this month that cybercriminals had gained access to the state's IT system for social services.
item.dc:creator: Ted Nesi, NewsNation
